acceptance_targets: [chapter_13_craft_001] Pass if, before the tackle at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:428, a cold reader can state why the thirty-thousand figure may not hold and what clock or decision threatens it, and the office scene at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:522 confirms that on-page logic. Fail if the first explanation for the reduced payout still appears only when the clerk says it at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:522.

[chapter_13_award_001] Pass if the closing span beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:587 leaves at least one explicit new obligation, decision, or piece of crew knowledge on page that a reader could name as forward pressure for the next chapter. Fail if the ending still resolves to only 'they're still broke, still strained, and marginally kinder.'

[chapter_13_dialogue_001] Pass if, in the revised briefing at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:92, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:104, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:110, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:184, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:194, no speaker gives a two-sentence thesis statement of their emotional position, Jet uses contractions except where emphasis clearly demands otherwise, and at least two exchanges show mistrust through interruption, refusal, or redirection instead of explanation.

[chapter_13_prose_001] Pass if no more than one of the cited passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:216, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:270, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:340, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:372, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:392, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:546, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:629 still uses a 'Not X ... / Because or Just Y' corrective construction, and the revised lines remain equally clear by direct reading.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, before the tackle at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:428, a cold reader can state why the thirty-thousand figure may not hold and what clock or decision threatens it, and the office scene at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:522 confirms that on-page logic. Fail if the first explanation for the reduced payout still appears only when the clerk says it at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:522.
findings.problem: The chapter's economic turn depends on a last-minute offstage bond reduction that arrives only at payout. Because the briefing and chase treat the thirty-thousand figure as stable fact, the cut to fifteen-eight reads less like earned consequence than authorial arithmetic imposed after the crew finally executes well, and the grocery coda inherits that convenience.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the setup at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:26 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:72 so the crew knows the poster may be stale or that a live bond-review clock is already running. Then revise the payout reveal at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:522 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:528 so the office scene pays off that setup, or rework the capture choice around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:408 so a specific on-page decision explains why the net drops.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the closing span beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:587 leaves at least one explicit new obligation, decision, or piece of crew knowledge on page that a reader could name as forward pressure for the next chapter. Fail if the ending still resolves to only 'they're still broke, still strained, and marginally kinder.'
findings.problem: The chapter proves the crew can still function together and closes on a graceful small gesture, but it stops at proof-of-function. Beyond Faye taking Jet's splitting bag, the ordeal does not produce a sharper decision, obligation, or revelation that materially changes the novel's pressure, so the chapter reads as strong connective tissue rather than a standout chapter in its own right.
findings.rewrite_direction: Strengthen the closing movement at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:587, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:609, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:653, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:659 so the reduced payout forces one concrete new condition between crew members. That condition can be financial, operational, or emotional, but it should arise from this ordeal and remain active after the walk back to the ship.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in the revised briefing at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:92, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:104, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:110, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:184, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:194, no speaker gives a two-sentence thesis statement of their emotional position, Jet uses contractions except where emphasis clearly demands otherwise, and at least two exchanges show mistrust through interruption, refusal, or redirection instead of explanation.
findings.problem: The opening briefing sometimes turns strained subtext into composed mini-speeches. Jet and Faye state the chapter's thesis outright ('We need the money. That's all this is.' / 'I'm doing this because I need money. Don't make it into anything else.'), and Jet's 'He does not grow it himself' slips into a stiffness that cuts against his high-contraction, pressured register. The scene stays legible, but the speech is cleaner and more literary than the pressure warrants.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the briefing around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:92, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:104, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:110, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:184, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:194 so bargaining over shares, route calls, and comm discipline carries the tension. Shorten Jet's plan talk, restore contractions where there is no deliberate emphasis, and let at least two beats of mistrust emerge through interruption, refusal, or subject-shift rather than direct self-explanation.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if no more than one of the cited passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:216, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:270, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:340, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:372, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:392, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:546, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:629 still uses a 'Not X ... / Because or Just Y' corrective construction, and the revised lines remain equally clear by direct reading.
findings.problem: A contrastive corrective sentence shape becomes a chapter-level tic: 'Not much. Enough to notice.' 'Not because he was scared exactly. Because...' 'Not graceful. Effective.' 'Not dramatic silence. Not movie silence.' Used this often, the narrator starts pre-interpreting moments instead of letting image and action do the work, and the rhythm begins to sound generated rather than chosen.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the cited moments at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:216, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:270, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:340, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:372, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:392, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:546, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:629 so most of them land through concrete physical detail, movement, or sensory residue instead of narrator-side correction. Keep at most one of these contrastive beats if you want it as a deliberate rhythmic choice.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise demands tonal range that most novels avoid: genuine slapstick comedy in the same book as genuine grief, syndicate violence alongside domestic warmth, existential identity crisis alongside arguments about noodles. The risk is not in the dark material — it's in the tonal juxtaposition. Commit to both registers fully. A comic scene should be actually funny, not wryly amusing. A grief scene should be actually painful, not tastefully sad. The Bebop register earns its power by refusing to choose between comedy and tragedy — they coexist in the same room, the same conversation, the same character. Do not smooth the transitions between registers. Let them be jarring when the material is jarring. Let a joke land in the middle of a painful conversation if that's what the character would do. Let a painful truth arrive during a comic scene without the comedy stopping to make room for it. The creative risk is trust: trust that the reader can hold both, trust that tonal instability is a form of emotional honesty, not a failure of control.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure. When a scene arrives at a moment of physical, emotional, or moral consequence, the prose must stay present — naming, rendering, inhabiting. Summary, vagueness, and decorative circumlocution at the point of impact are disallowed. If a character is hurt, drunk, broke, grieving, or afraid, render the specific texture of that experience. 'She was devastated' is not writing. 'She found his grocery list on the fridge' is writing. The Bebop world is broke, dangerous, and populated by people who have lost things they cannot get back. The prose must honor that.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific precision. The Bebop crew lives in contractions; full forms should feel conspicuous when they appear.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Consecutive chapters must differ in at least two of: scene count, dialogue density, pacing, primary emotional register, and structural shape. A dialogue-heavy chapter should be followed by one with more interiority or action. A multi-scene chapter should be followed by one with fewer scenes or a different temporal rhythm. Vary paragraph length distribution across chapters — some chapters should run long and flowing, others should be built from shorter, harder units. The novel's rhythm at the chapter level should feel like a jazz set: different tempos, different keys, with throughlines that connect them.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, vernacular-adjacent, unsentimental. The narration should sound like an extension of the characters' world — working-class, spacefaring, slightly retro-future. Avoid elevated literary diction unless the passage specifically calls for it (rare). Avoid clinical or academic language. The narrator is not above the characters; the narrator is in the room with them. Latinate vocabulary should be rare and purposeful. Anglo-Saxon roots preferred for physical description.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating by chapter or scene as indicated in the outline. The camera sits behind the POV character's eyes — we see what they see, hear their internal register in the narration, and do not access other characters' thoughts except through observed behavior. When the chapter is ensemble, the camera moves between characters within scenes but does not overlay multiple interiorities simultaneously. Maintain enough distance that the narrator's voice is present alongside the character's — this is not stream of consciousness but inhabited third person.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Jazz-inflected variation. Sentences should vary aggressively in length and structure — short declaratives next to longer compound sentences, fragments where they create rhythmic force (but never elliptical clipping). The Bebop register favors syncopation: beat expectations set up and then broken. Avoid metronomic alternation (short-long-short-long). Let passages breathe with their own rhythms. Action sequences should accelerate sentence pace. Reflective passages should slow and lengthen. Transitions between registers should be controlled, not abrupt.
